articles.title: Clinical presentation and outcomes in non-maltophilia Stenotrophomonas spp. bloodstream infections
articles.summary: Scientific Reports, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41598-026-59102-9 Clinical presentation and outcomes in non-maltophilia Stenotrophomonas spp. bloodstream infections
articles.content:
Abstract

Stenotrophomonas maltophilia complex is an important nosocomial cause of bacteraemia. The complex comprises multiple species, but their relative clinical and genomic significance remains unclear. We examined clinical and genomic differences between S. maltophilia and non-S. maltophilia species to better understand their virulence and implications for patient care. Hospitalized adult patients (≥ 21 years) between January 2022 and June 2024 with Stenotrophomonas species bacteraemia were examined. Clinical data were extracted from electronic medical records. Whole-genome sequencing was performed on all isolates, with species assignment confirmed by average nucleotide identity. S. maltophilia and non-S. maltophilia species were compared. Fifty-three S. maltophilia complex isolates were identified, of which 45 had clinical data. S. maltophilia predominated (n = 32), followed by S. pavanii (n = 7), S. sepilia (n = 4), S. muris (n = 1), and S. geniculata (n = 1). S. maltophilia isolates formed multiple clades with diverse sequence types, consistent with both clonal expansion and ongoing diversification. Virulence genes, including stmPr, smoR, and iron acquisition-associated genes were conserved across S. maltophilia lineages, but stmPr1 was less frequently observed in non-S. maltophilia. Most infections were line-related. Pneumonia-associated bacteraemia appeared more frequently among with S. maltophilia infections. Polymicrobial bacteraemia appeared more commonly in non-S. maltophilia infections. Mortality was similar between groups, but recurrence of infection within one year occurred only in S. maltophilia. Compared with non-S. maltophilia, S. maltophilia appeared to be associated with pneumonia and recurrent bacteraemia episodes, although mortality was similar. These exploratory findings suggest potential differences within the complex, but require validation in larger studies.

articles.title: Towards million-token context windows: a topology-preserving framework for adaptive transformer sparsification
articles.summary: Scientific Reports, Published online: 06 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41598-026-59160-z Towards million-token context windows: a topology-preserving framework for adaptive transformer sparsification
articles.content:
Abstract

Transformer self-attention and billion-node network analyses share a key limitation: all-to-all evaluation creates an \(O(N^2)\) computational cost. Existing methods address this by either distributing the workload across hardware or substituting recurrent operators. This trades associative recall for efficiency. We present Reduced Interaction Sampling (RIS), a stochastic sparsification framework. RIS computes only a fraction of possible pairwise interactions. By leveraging topological redundancy in real-world networks, RIS separates structural accuracy from computational expense. For example, on the com-LiveJournal graph with 4 million nodes, RIS preserves the degree centrality rank (\(\rho = 0.96\)) while using only 10% of the edges. A partition-based setup, RIS-Structural, identifies twice as many hubs as sliding-window methods under heavy sparsity (1.00% vs 0.50%, \(p=0.033\)). In TinyLlama-1.1B attention tests (0.5k–65k tokens), RIS achieves a geometric reach of about 21k tokens at 65k–outperforming Longformer (\(\approx\)2k) and BigBird (\(\approx\)17k). Window-based models surpass \(10^5\) Cumulative Attention Mass but lose 98% of hub recovery. This shows that dense scalar weights poorly reflect long-range geometric reach. RIS maintains a stable Hub Recall with up to 128 times longer sequences and an edge budget below 0.01%. Stochastic sampling provides a mathematically robust way to scale context architectures without structural collapse.

Similar content being viewed by others

The author is grateful to the Federal University of Uberlândia (UFU) for providing the academic environment and the computational resources that enabled this independent research.

The author declares that no funds, grants, or other financial support were received during the preparation of this manuscript.

Author information

Authors and Affiliations

Corresponding author

Ethics declarations

Code availability

The source code for the Reduced Interaction Sampling (RIS) framework, including all benchmark scripts and implementation details, is available as a reproducible code capsule on the Code Ocean platform. This includes the core library ris_core.py, the validation suite, and all necessary dependencies to reproduce the results reported in this study. The executable capsule is publicly available under the Apache License 2.0 via its permanent DOI (https://doi.org/10.24433/CO.9986683.v1) and GitHub repository (https://github.com/santosardr/ris).

articles.title: Show HN: Reading Assistant Physical Books Meta RayBans
articles.summary: Reading technical books is hard. Unknown words, confusing phrases, and misunderstood concepts requires opening your phone, starting Claude, and working with Sonnet through a series of exchanges, before finally getting it. Focusing back on reading after such a context switch ca...
articles.content:
Reading technical books is hard. Unknown words, confusing phrases, and misunderstood concepts requires opening your phone, starting Claude, and working with Sonnet through a series of exchanges, before finally getting it.

Focusing back on reading after such a context switch can be difficult. Repeated multiple times and your attention span is gone.

Fix: An assistant understands your book deeply and answers inquiries through voice while you read, side-stepping the current process entirely.

All you have to do is: - Upload a PDF version of your physical book and start reading - Say "Hey Lumos" along with your inquiry ("why does det(A) mean the matrix loses volume?") - Assistant researches the contents of the book and responds - Continue reading until you need help again

It's in extreme beta but works well enough where I can't read a book without it. If you're interested in trying it out, reach out.

articles.title: China’s ByteDance discovers new scaling law that could sustain AI boom
articles.summary: Researchers at TikTok parent ByteDance have discovered a new scaling law governing how fast artificial intelligence agents can improve by performing real-world tasks, a finding that could help prolong the AI boom just as traditional development methods hit a wall. In a researc...
articles.content:
China’s ByteDance discovers new scaling law that could sustain AI boom

Researchers at the Chinese tech giant have been analysing how fast AI agents can improve by performing real-world tasks

In a research paper published on Thursday, ByteDance’s Seed AI team revealed that AI agents – autonomous software that executes tasks on a human’s behalf – can double their learning speed every three months by interacting with real-world environments over extended periods.

The finding comes as the global AI industry searches for new ways to improve models. For years, developers relied on feeding systems more data and computing power during initial training, but prominent industry figures – including OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy – have warned that this brute-force approach cannot last forever.

However, despite the fact that tech firms are pivoting towards agentic AI, ByteDance researchers noted in the paper that how these autonomous systems “learn from real-world environments after deployment remains far less understood”.

To address the problem, the team developed EdgeBench, a benchmarking suite featuring 134 ultra-long-horizon tasks spanning a wide range of areas from software engineering and scientific discovery to formal mathematics and professional knowledge work. Each task requires at least 12 hours of continuous AI agent operation.

articles.title: Has CSX (CSX) Outpaced Other Transportation Stocks This Year?
articles.summary: CSX (CSX) has outperformed the broader Transportation sector and its specific Rail industry year-to-date, with a 34.9% return compared to the sector's 16.6% average. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy), supported by a 3.1% increase in its full-year earnings co...
articles.content:
For those looking to find strong Transportation stocks, it is prudent to search for companies in the group that are outperforming their peers. Is CSX (CSX) one of those stocks right now? A quick glance at the company's year-to-date performance in comparison to the rest of the Transportation sector should help us answer this question.

CSX is one of 110 individual stocks in the Transportation sector. Collectively, these companies sit at #2 in the Zacks Sector Rank. The Zacks Sector Rank gauges the strength of our 16 individual sector groups by measuring the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within the groups.

The Zacks Rank emphasizes earnings estimates and estimate revisions to find stocks with improving earnings outlooks. This system has a long record of success, and these stocks tend to be on track to beat the market over the next one to three months. CSX is currently sporting a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy).

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for CSX's full-year earnings has moved 3.1% higher within the past quarter. This is a sign of improving analyst sentiment and a positive earnings outlook trend.

According to our latest data, CSX has moved about 34.9% on a year-to-date basis. Meanwhile, the Transportation sector has returned an average of 16.6% on a year-to-date basis. As we can see, CSX is performing better than its sector in the calendar year.

One other Transportation stock that has outperformed the sector so far this year is C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW). The stock is up 18.1% year-to-date.

Over the past three months, C.H. Robinson Worldwide's consensus EPS estimate for the current year has increased 3.2%. The stock currently has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

Looking more specifically, CSX belongs to the Transportation - Rail industry, a group that includes 9 individual stocks and currently sits at #105 in the Zacks Industry Rank. This group has gained an average of 22.4% so far this year, so CSX is performing better in this area.

C.H. Robinson Worldwide, however, belongs to the Transportation - Services industry. Currently, this 20-stock industry is ranked #156. The industry has moved +11% so far this year.

Investors with an interest in Transportation stocks should continue to track CSX and C.H. Robinson Worldwide. These stocks will be looking to continue their solid performance.

articles.title: Extra Space Storage stock holds as investors watch execution.
articles.summary: Extra Space Storage Inc. (ISIN US30225T1025) operates as a major U.S. self-storage landlord, generating revenue from rental income and related services. The company's performance is closely watched by investors within the broader U.S. REIT market, with a focus on its ability t...
articles.content:
By Christina Vaughn, Background & Management desk. Reviewed on July 3, 2026 at 4:18 p.m. ET.

Extra Space Storage, Inc. (ISIN US30225T1025) remains a major U.S. self-storage landlord with a portfolio built around operating storage facilities and related services. The company trades on the NYSE, which keeps it firmly within the U.S. large-cap real estate universe.

Business model

Extra Space Storage earns its revenue from rental income on storage units and from services tied to storage operations. That model gives investors a direct read on occupancy, pricing power, and local demand across a broad U.S. footprint.

Market context

Shares are being priced in the context of the wider U.S. REIT market, where rates, financing costs, and consumer demand remain central to valuation. For Extra Space Storage, the key question is how consistently its portfolio can translate that backdrop into stable cash generation.

Storage services

Extra Space Storage also offers services tied to moving and storage needs, which broadens the customer relationship beyond unit rental alone. That combination helps make the business more than a simple warehouse owner.

Price context

As of July 3, 2026 at 4:18 p.m. ET, a verified live quote was not available in the current source set, so the article centers on the company profile and market context. NYSE-listed Extra Space Storage remains a closely watched U.S. REIT.

articles.title: Weyerhaeuser builds value in timber and real estate operations
articles.summary: Weyerhaeuser Co. operates a large timberland and wood products platform in North America, integrating forestry assets with a real estate and natural resources portfolio. This business model is designed to generate resilient cash flows across various economic cycles by balancin...
articles.content: Weyerhaeuser Co. operates a large timberland and wood products platform in North America, integrating forestry assets with a real estate and natural resources portfolio. This business model is designed to generate resilient cash flows across various economic cycles by balancing current demand with long-term resource stewardship and capitalizing on real estate development opportunities. The company's integrated structure allows it to optimize timber supply, wood product manufacturing, and land monetization, providing a distinctive profile for investors interested in land, natural resources, and real estate.

articles.title: Claude Fable 5 Isn't Nerfed. The Router Is Just Paranoid
articles.summary: Did Claude Fable 5 get dumber? Two benchmarks, two wildly different conclusions—and one routing layer that explains the whole mess.
articles.content:
In brief

Have been using Fable 5 all day just continuing what I was doing with Opus

The findings are true

It's completely nerfed

Politics has nuked civilian technological advancement once again https://t.co/Ed3jrqOxbK

— BharadwajC (@bwjbuild) July 2, 2026

What BridgeBench actually measured

FABLE 5 CAME BACK NERFED.

We re-ran the July 1st version of Claude Fable 5 on BridgeBench.

The results are brutal:

Debugging: 86.2 → 25.9


Refactoring: 73.6 → 38.4
Hallucination: 75.9 → 61.7

The new guardrails are kicking in on way too many tasks and falling back to Opus… pic.twitter.com/tcUDDXpZMF

— BridgeMind (@bridgemindai) July 2, 2026

What Arena.AI actually measured

The community has been asking how Claude Fable 5 compares before vs. after its latest re-deployment.

We collected thousands of votes on the new endpoint across Arenas - Text, Vision, Document, Code, and Agent - and here’s an early score preview.

So far, scores look mostly… https://t.co/FKDaPpz10e pic.twitter.com/1nJDHqnlIj

— Arena.ai (@arena) July 2, 2026

articles.title: Camera with transparent display launches for the equivalent of $29
articles.summary: Most compact cameras use a conventional display as a viewfinder, while some models also offer an optical or electronic viewfinder. The Godox C100, however, takes a different approach, as this compact camera features a large viewing window that doubles as a transparent display....
articles.content:
Most compact cameras use a conventional display as a viewfinder, while some models also offer an optical or electronic viewfinder. The Godox C100, however, takes a different approach, as this compact camera features a large viewing window that doubles as a transparent display.


This screen displays details about the battery, the selected mode, and the exposure settings, while small corner elements hint at what will ultimately appear in the photo. Photos and videos can be recorded in 1:1, 3:2, 4:3, and 16:9 aspect ratios. Photos are stored on a microSD card with a maximum capacity of 128 GB and can be transferred to a smartphone via USB-C. Godox has apparently omitted Wi-Fi and Bluetooth for cost reasons.

While Godox does not provide any details about the camera’s hardware, this very affordable camera, weighing just 65 grams, is unlikely to take photos much better than toy cameras like the Kodak Charmera ($35 on Amazon). At least for photographers, however, the Godox C100 is meant to be more than just a toy, because at the touch of a button, the camera can be transformed into a light meter that indicates the correct exposure at the center of the frame. Among other things, this is useful for setting the correct exposure on an analog camera that doesn’t have a built-in light meter.

Pricing and availability

The Godox C100 compact camera is set to launch first in China, priced at CNY 199 (approx. $29). Godox has not yet confirmed any details regarding the international launch, but Godox products are typically available internationally as well. More details about the C100 are available on the official product page.

articles.title: Kimco Realty Corp highlights retail-focused REIT strategy as investors eye stable income
articles.summary: Kimco Realty Corp, a significant retail-focused real estate investment trust (REIT) in the U.S., emphasizes its strategy centered on open-air shopping centers anchored by grocery and necessity-based tenants. The company aims to provide stable rental income and regular dividend...
articles.content:
Kimco Realty Corp (ISIN US49338L1035) is a large publicly traded real estate investment trust in the United States that focuses on ownership and operation of open-air shopping centers and mixed-use properties. The company generates revenue primarily from leasing space to a broad mix of retail tenants, with an emphasis on grocery and other necessity-based categories that tend to be more resilient across economic cycles. As a retail-focused REIT, Kimco Realty Corp aims to deliver steady rental income and regular dividends to its shareholders.

Retail real estate and tenant mix

Kimco Realty Corp structures its portfolio around shopping centers that generally feature grocery anchors, discount retailers, restaurants and service-oriented businesses. This tenant mix is designed to attract consistent foot traffic, which can support occupancy levels and long-term lease relationships. The company typically enters into multi-year leases with its tenants, providing predictable cash flows that can underpin its distribution policy for shareholders.

The REIT model used by Kimco Realty Corp requires that it distribute a significant portion of its taxable income to investors in the form of dividends. This framework encourages disciplined capital allocation and active management of occupancy rates, rental levels and operating costs. For many retail investors, the combination of real estate-backed assets and recurring dividend payments is a central part of the appeal of Kimco Realty Corp.

Financial focus and balance sheet

As a real estate investment trust, Kimco Realty Corp places considerable emphasis on metrics such as funds from operations, net operating income and leverage ratios. These measures help investors evaluate the company’s ability to sustain and grow its dividend over time while maintaining a balance sheet that can support reinvestment in properties, redevelopment projects and selective acquisitions. Management typically seeks to diversify funding sources across equity and debt, aiming to keep borrowing costs aligned with market conditions.

Kimco Realty Corp’s strategy includes reinvesting in existing properties to enhance tenant appeal, improve layouts and adapt to evolving retail formats. Redevelopment can range from modest interior upgrades to larger projects that add residential or office components to existing shopping centers. Such mixed-use initiatives are intended to deepen property utilization, create new income streams and strengthen the long-term value of the assets.

Business model and REIT structure

Kimco Realty Corp’s business model centers on acquiring, owning and actively managing income-producing retail properties. The company earns base rent from tenants, as well as potential percentage rent or other variable components linked to tenant sales in some leases. In addition, it may receive income from ancillary services such as signage, parking rights or co-tenancy arrangements, all of which can contribute to property-level returns.

Under the REIT structure, Kimco Realty Corp is designed to avoid corporate income tax at the entity level by meeting requirements such as focusing on real estate assets and distributing most taxable income to shareholders. This can make the vehicle tax-efficient for certain investors, while aligning the company’s strategy squarely with real estate operations and rental performance. The REIT designation also shapes how the company reports key performance metrics, especially those related to cash flow and recurring operations.

Risk management is a core component of Kimco Realty Corp’s approach. The company typically strives to diversify its tenant base by category and credit quality and to spread properties across multiple geographic areas. This diversification is intended to reduce exposure to localized economic downturns or sector-specific challenges within retail. Lease structures may include provisions to protect the landlord in cases of tenant default while also offering flexibility to bring in new tenants as consumer preferences evolve.

Representative property concept

A representative Kimco Realty Corp property is an open-air shopping center anchored by a national or regional grocery store, with additional tenants such as pharmacies, quick-service restaurants, fitness operators, discount apparel and local services. These centers are often located in suburban or urban infill locations where household density and traffic patterns support recurring visits. The open-air format allows for relatively lower operating costs compared with enclosed malls and can facilitate incremental changes in tenant configuration as new concepts emerge.

From the tenant perspective, locating in a Kimco Realty Corp center offers access to established consumer traffic and co-tenancy with complementary brands. For the landlord, strong anchors can help keep smaller shop spaces occupied by providing a reliable demand driver. The mix of national chains and local businesses can reduce reliance on any single customer segment and foster a more resilient ecosystem within the property.

Kimco Realty Corp stock and investor perspective

Kimco Realty Corp stock represents ownership in a portfolio of retail real estate assets that generate income through long-term leasing. Investors often evaluate the shares based on dividend yield, payout sustainability, property quality and management’s track record in capital allocation. As with other REITs, share performance is influenced not only by company-specific factors but also by interest rates, broader economic conditions and sentiment toward commercial real estate as an asset class.

Because Kimco Realty Corp is publicly traded, its stock price reflects ongoing market assessments of future rental income, occupancy trends and potential growth from redevelopment or acquisitions. For income-oriented investors, the key consideration is whether the company can continue to support its dividend while navigating changes in retail formats and consumer habits. For more growth-focused investors, opportunities arising from portfolio optimization and new development projects may be particularly relevant.

This article was generated automatically and technically reviewed before publication. Market prices, analyst data and company information are provided without warranty and may change at short notice. articles.title: General Electric (GE) Could Be 8% Overvalued As Jet Engine Demand Strengthens
articles.summary: General Electric (GE) is currently estimated to be 8% overvalued based on a fair value calculation of $350.45 against its last closing price of $377.52, despite strong demand in its GE Aerospace division. The company has seen significant stock momentum with a 53.73% return ove...
articles.content:
General Electric (GE) is back in focus after GE Aerospace reported strong demand for commercial jet engines and services, supported by major airline orders and long term agreements that underpin its sizeable backlog.

General Electric has seen strong momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 18.82% and a 90 day share price return of 34.27%, while its 1 year total shareholder return of 53.73% and very large 5 year total shareholder return suggest investors have been rewarded for staying invested.

If you are tracking how aerospace focused companies like GE Aerospace are trading, it can also be useful to widen your watchlist and look at 35 power grid technology and infrastructure stocks

After such strong recent returns and enthusiasm around GE Aerospace backlog and earnings, the key question now is valuation: is General Electric still trading below what its fundamentals might justify, or is the market already pricing in years of future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 7.7% Overvalued

General Electric last closed at $377.52, while the most followed narrative frames a fair value of $350.45 using an 8.0% discount rate and detailed long term projections.

Major supply chain stabilization and productivity gains from the FLIGHT DECK operating model and $2B+ investment in capacity are unlocking pent-up services demand and enabling double-digit output growth, translating into sustained higher free cash flow conversion and improved operating leverage.

Curious what kind of revenue path, margin expansion, and future earnings multiple are needed to back that fair value for General Electric? The narrative relies on a specific blend of growth assumptions, profitability targets, and share count changes that paints a very particular picture of GE Aerospace cash generation.

Result: Fair Value of $350.45 (OVERVALUED)

However, even for General Electric, a prolonged air travel slowdown or persistent supply chain and cost pressures could quickly challenge the upbeat cash flow story behind this narrative.

Next Steps

If this General Electric story sounds convincing or too optimistic, promptly review the underlying numbers yourself and consider both sides of the debate with the 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign

articles.title: 'Baseless': Cair sues Florida governor for terrorism designation alongside drug cartels
articles.summary: 'Baseless': Cair sues Florida governor for terrorism designation alongside drug cartels Republican governors in both Florida and Texas have made the Muslim civil rights group a particular target Florida Governor Ron DeSantis speaks to reporters at the South Florida Detention F...
articles.content:
'Baseless': Cair sues Florida governor for terrorism designation alongside drug cartels

The largest Muslim civil rights group in the US has sued the governor of Florida for designating it a "domestic terrorist organisation", potentially alongside some of the most deadly drug cartels in the Americas.

The Council on American-Islamic Relations (Cair) filed the lawsuit on Thursday in partnership with non-profits The American Civil Liberties Union and the Southern Poverty Law Center, calling the new law "baseless".

The designation opens up Cair to "immediate and irreparable harm, including the shuttering of all their operations and advocacy in the state", the group said in a statement.

Florida has further listed the Muslim Brotherhood, which has no known operation in the US or a centralised headquarters globally, as a terrorist organisation, as well as "antifa", which is short for the anti-fascist movement. It also has no defined scope or location.

"Officials can brand nonprofit corporations with debilitating stigma and then use an array of state authorities to immediately silence and incapacitate the organization, its employees, its members, and a wide range of others associated with the group through extraordinarily broad and severe criminal, civil, and administrative penalties," Cair said.

Governor Ron DeSantis has recommended that 90 other groups fall under the new terrorism law, including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, the Venezuela-based criminal gang Tren de Aragua, and the deadly Mexico-based Sinaloa Cartel.

The designation stems from HB 1471, which was passed by the Florida legislature in March. DeSantis signed it in April, and it went into effect on 1 July.

DeSantis had previously tried to go it alone, signing an executive order in December to blacklist Cair, but a federal judge blocked it earlier this year.

“The question before this Court is whether the Governor can, in a non-emergency situation, unilaterally designate one of the largest Muslim civil rights groups in America as a ‘terrorist organization’ and withhold government benefits from anyone providing material support or resources to the group,” judge Mark Walker wrote in his order.

“This Court finds he cannot.”

Cair is now seeking an emergency injunction to stop the enforcement of HB 1471.

HB 1471's language indicates that providing support to designated terrorist groups will lead to criminal liability, and ensures that religious codes - sometimes protected by the First Amendment - do not supersede Florida law.

Cair and its partners said in the lawsuit that the designation model "contains no requirement that notice to the designated group include all the reasons for the designation and all the material evidence—inculpatory and exculpatory—that state decisionmakers may rely on to make or approve the designation. It does not require the State to prove or even provide evidence that the organization violated any law".

Muslim Brotherhood

Cair is a US-based non-profit organisation that advocates for civil liberties and relies on donations to carry out its work.

While executive orders against it were in effect in both Florida and Texas, Cair was prevented from buying land and from engaging in government contracts, Edward Ahmed Mitchell, Cair's deputy director in Washington, DC, previously told Middle East Eye.

In both states, the Muslim Brotherhood was also labelled a terrorist organisation.

Republicans, most vocally led by Texas Senator Ted Cruz, have said the Muslim Brotherhood maintains influential ties to Cair and is out to impose Sharia law on Americans.

Cair has long faced those accusations, but it maintains that its budget is overwhelmingly derived from donations by the American public, Muslim or otherwise.

The Muslim Brotherhood is currently listed as a terrorist group in the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Syria, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia.

There is also a US terrorism designation at the federal level of Muslim Brotherhood chapters in Egypt, Jordan, and Lebanon, as ordered by President Donald Trump.

Critics have long insisted that blacklisting the political movement is a way of ensuring it will no longer pose a threat to monarchies and authoritarian leaders in the Middle East.

Cruz said the Muslim Brotherhood - whose popularity remains high in parts of the Arab world because of its combination of Islamic religious tradition, political activism and social welfare programming - is a threat to US national security.

Middle East Eye previously put the question to George Washington University professor Nathan Brown, who is an expert on Middle East politics and sits on the board of trustees at the American University in Cairo.

In what ways is the Muslim Brotherhood a threat to US national security?

"None at all," he said.

articles.title: Zions Bancorp focuses on regional banking strength as investors watch credit quality
articles.summary: Zions Bancorp, a regional bank in the Western U.S., is under investor scrutiny for its credit quality, loan growth, and deposit trends amidst the current interest rate environment. The bank focuses on a relationship-driven model for small to mid-sized commercial clients, offer...
articles.content:
Zions Bancorp (ISIN US9897011071) is a long-established regional bank holding company in the United States, known for its network of banking subsidiaries serving businesses and households across several Western states. The group operates a relationship-driven banking model that emphasizes small and mid-sized commercial clients, real estate lending, and a full suite of retail services. For investors, the way Zions Bancorp navigates the current interest rate environment and credit conditions is central to the long-term earnings story.

Regional banking profile and footprint

Zions Bancorp traces its roots back more than a century and has grown into a multi-bank organization with operations concentrated in the Western U.S. Economic activity in these markets is closely tied to sectors such as construction, real estate development, energy-related services, and a broad range of small businesses. The company’s strategy relies on deep local ties, with regional brands that retain a significant degree of autonomy while benefiting from centralized risk management and technology platforms. This multi-brand structure allows Zions Bancorp to tailor products and services to local market needs while maintaining scale efficiencies in back-office operations.

The bank’s deposit base is diversified across retail customers, small businesses, and larger commercial relationships. A sizable share of deposits tends to be noninterest-bearing checking balances and operational accounts tied to business customers. These balances can be a valuable low-cost funding source when interest rates are higher, supporting the net interest margin that is a core driver of profitability for a traditional lender. At the same time, competition for deposits from money market funds and large national institutions can be intense, encouraging the bank to continuously evaluate its pricing and product offerings.

Earnings drivers and risk management focus

For Zions Bancorp, net interest income remains the foundation of earnings, reflecting the spread between yields on loans and securities and the cost of funding those assets. Loan portfolios typically include commercial and industrial lending, commercial real estate exposure, and consumer credit such as residential mortgages and home equity products. The performance of these portfolios is sensitive to trends in business investment, employment, and property values in the regions the bank serves.

Credit quality is another central theme that investors track closely. As the economic cycle evolves, regional banks like Zions Bancorp monitor delinquency rates, nonperforming loans, and net charge-offs to assess whether borrowers are experiencing stress. Provisions for credit losses can fluctuate meaningfully over time, depending on management’s assessment of future economic conditions and specific portfolio risks. A disciplined approach to underwriting and concentration limits is important in moderating volatility in earnings and capital.

Capital and liquidity metrics play a critical role in the bank’s resilience. Regulatory ratios such as common equity tier 1 capital and risk-based capital are monitored by supervisors to ensure that the company can absorb losses under adverse scenarios. Zions Bancorp also maintains liquidity buffers through cash, high-quality securities, and committed funding lines so that it can meet obligations even during periods of market disruption. For shareholders, a stable capital position underpins the potential for dividends and, when appropriate, share repurchases authorized by the board.

Business mix and technology initiatives

Beyond traditional lending and deposit services, Zions Bancorp generates fee income across areas such as treasury management, card services, and wealth advisory. These revenue streams can help diversify earnings away from purely interest-rate-driven factors. Cash management offerings often include electronic payment solutions, remote deposit capture, lockbox services, and online portals designed to give business customers real-time visibility into their accounts. Over time, expanding these services and deepening client relationships can support both fee income and core deposit growth.

Technology investment has become increasingly important for regional banks seeking to compete with national players and digital-only challengers. Zions Bancorp continues to modernize its online and mobile banking platforms, aiming to offer intuitive interfaces, integrated financial tools, and secure authentication methods. Digital onboarding for new accounts, streamlined loan applications, and data-driven credit decisioning are examples of areas where technology can improve customer experience while lowering operating costs.

Cybersecurity and operational resilience are also key priorities. As more transactions move to digital channels, the bank must safeguard customer data and payment flows against evolving threats. Investments in monitoring systems, staff training, and incident response frameworks are part of a broader effort to maintain trust and comply with regulatory expectations. These efforts are not directly visible in headline financial metrics but form the foundation for sustainable growth.

Representative product: commercial lending and treasury services

A representative product set for Zions Bancorp is the combination of commercial lending and associated treasury management services for small and mid-sized enterprises. Businesses can apply for credit facilities tailored to working capital needs, equipment purchases, real estate expansion, or acquisition financing. Loan structures often include revolving lines of credit, term loans, and letters of credit, with pricing that reflects borrower creditworthiness, collateral quality, and duration.

Alongside lending, treasury services help clients manage daily cash flows efficiently. This may include automated clearing house payment solutions, wire transfers, account reconciliation tools, and fraud prevention features such as positive pay. By bundling credit products with these services, Zions Bancorp can strengthen client relationships and better understand the financial profile of its customers, which in turn supports risk management and cross-selling opportunities.

Stock trading context and investor perspective

Zions Bancorp stock is listed in the United States and trades in U.S. dollars, offering investors exposure to the regional banking segment of the financial sector. The share price reflects market expectations for future earnings, dividend capacity, and the outlook for credit conditions across the bank’s core markets. As interest rates move and regulatory requirements evolve, regional bank valuations can shift to reflect perceived changes in profitability and resilience.

For investors evaluating Zions Bancorp, key themes typically include loan growth trends, deposit stability, capital adequacy, and management’s stance on balancing shareholder returns with prudential buffers. Comparisons with other regional banks can help frame how the company is positioned from a risk and reward standpoint. Over longer horizons, the ability to adapt to technological change, maintain strong customer relationships, and respond to shifts in the economic environment is likely to shape returns.

This article was generated automatically and technically reviewed before publication. Market prices, analyst data and company information are provided without warranty and may change at short notice. This content is for informational purposes only and is not investment, financial, legal or tax advice. It is not a recommendation to buy or sell any security. Investing in securities involves risk, including the possible loss of principal.

articles.title: Show HN: Track Token usage for major platforms,know your token flow
articles.summary: I use multiple AI tools for work and also my side projects, and the annoying part was to track my costs and token usage across tools. Everytime I had to visit each tool and its respective usage setting to check it and I was losing patience and also was getting hit by surprise...
articles.content:
I use multiple AI tools for work and also my side projects, and the annoying part was to track my costs and token usage across tools. Everytime I had to visit each tool and its respective usage setting to check it and I was losing patience and also was getting hit by surprise limits

Now I know that there are already free/open-source trackers for Cursor or Claude usage, and they are useful if that is all you need.

My problem is broader as I wanted one small place to see tokens, spend, subscriptions and limits across the AI tools I actually use. I was really tired of switching tabs and apps to check the usage.

So I built Tokens 4 Breakfast

It sits in the Mac menu bar and helps track usage across Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, OpenAI API, Copilot and other providers. You can also set limit reminders and focus mode to always stay under the budget.

Check your usage insights per project and per model in Claude Code to get a even better understanding of where your tokens are flowing plus many more interesting features.

Local-first. No login. No cloud. No telemetry. No subscriptions. Privacy Focused.

Built in

Not trying to replace individual app dashboard but just trying to stop jumping between five different places while building worrying about hitting limits and exceeding costs.

Would genuinely love feedback from you all Power users.

Thank you all in advance.

articles.title: Show HN: I ran 400 hours of interviews, so I built the tool I wished existed
articles.summary: I've spent almost a decade at one of the FAANG companies, and I've conducted over 400 hours of software engineering interviews. The frustration led me to build ScreenStack. You see, the interviewing stack today is fragmented. To conduct an interview, you'll have to schedule a...
articles.content:
I've spent almost a decade at one of the FAANG companies, and I've conducted over 400 hours of software engineering interviews. The frustration led me to build ScreenStack.

You see, the interviewing stack today is fragmented. To conduct an interview, you'll have to schedule a conference call on Zoom/Google Meet/MS Teams, then have a CoderPad session, and, most recently, connect your AI note-taker to capture notes. After the interview, it is an open secret that a lot of people paste the transcripts into ChatGPT and sometimes the code too and from there try to generate post-interview notes to send to the hiring manager or debrief team.

You can already see how crazy this looks. I strongly believe that everything you need to conduct an interview should be in one place: the transcript, the code, and/or the diagram (for system design interviews). So that when you want to extract valuable insights using an LLM, you have enough context about what they said, at what time and what code they produced. This is not to say the LLM makes the decision, but it helps you analyse and get a clearer picture of how the interview went so you are making hiring decisions not based on vibes but actual data.

I built ScreenStack, an MVP as a proof of concept, and now I'm looking for design partners. If you are currently conducting coding interviews or any interview at all and would like to try it out, please shoot me an email at screenstackhq [at] gmail dot com. I'll be happy to walk you through how it works, and hopefully you use it to conduct some interviews entirely for free; my only ask is that you give me feedback.

Thank you in advance.

articles.title: Nasa launches robotic mission to save space telescope
articles.summary: Nasa launched a robotic mission on Friday to try to prevent one of its ageing telescopes from burning up in the atmosphere, a complicated operation expected to last several months. The unprecedented US$30 million effort involves sending a robot to rescue the Swift space telesc...
articles.content:
Nasa launches robotic mission to save space telescope

A successful bid to stop the Swift telescope from burning up in the atmosphere could pave the way for giving other satellites a second life

Nasa launched a robotic mission on Friday to try to prevent one of its ageing telescopes from burning up in the atmosphere, a complicated operation expected to last several months.

The unprecedented US$30 million effort involves sending a robot to rescue the Swift space telescope that is currently falling towards Earth.

If successful, the mission could pave the way for giving other satellites a second life.

Initially scheduled for Tuesday, the robot’s launch was postponed due to weather and then technical issues. It finally took place on Friday at 0836 GMT from an atoll in the Pacific Ocean.

Developed by American start-up Katalyst, the spacecraft was launched by a small rocket named Pegasus, itself launched from an aeroplane.

Once it reaches an orbit close to that of Swift, the robot will deploy its solar panels and perform a series of checks.
